textEn: The Inevitable Hour!
What is the Inevitable Hour?
How do you know what the Inevitable Hour is?
Thamūd and ‘Ād denied the Striking Calamity of the Hour.
As for Thamūd, they were destroyed by a dreadful blast.

textAr: وَأَمَّا عَادٌ فَأُهْلِكُوا۟ بِرِيحٍ صَرْصَرٍ عَاتِيَةٍ
textEn: And as for ‘Ād, they were destroyed by a furious windstorm
textTranslit: Wa ammaa 'Aadun fa uhlikoo bireehin sarsarin 'aatiyah

textAr: سَخَّرَهَا عَلَيْهِمْ سَبْعَ لَيَالٍ وَثَمَـٰنِيَةَ أَيَّامٍ حُسُومًا فَتَرَى ٱلْقَوْمَ فِيهَا صَرْعَىٰ كَأَنَّهُمْ أَعْجَازُ نَخْلٍ خَاوِيَةٍ
textEn: which He unleashed against them for seven successive nights and eight days, so you would see the people lying dead as if they were hollow trunks of palm trees.
textTranslit: Sakhkharahaa 'alaihim sab'a la yaalinw wa samaaniyata ayyaamin husooman fataral qawma feehaa sar'aa ka annahum a'jaazu nakhlin khaawiyah

textEn: Do you see any trace of them now?

textAr: وَجَآءَ فِرْعَوْنُ وَمَن قَبْلَهُۥ وَٱلْمُؤْتَفِكَـٰتُ بِٱلْخَاطِئَةِ
textEn: Also, Pharaoh and those who came before him, and the overturned cities [of Lot] committed the grave sin,
textTranslit: Wa jaaa'a Firawnu wa man qablahoo wal mu'tafikaatu bilhaati'ah

textAr: فَعَصَوْا۟ رَسُولَ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَخَذَهُمْ أَخْذَةً رَّابِيَةً
textEn: each disobeying the messenger of their Lord, so He seized them with a severe punishment.
textTranslit: Fa'ansaw Rasoola Rabbihim fa akhazahum akhzatar raabiyah

textAr: إِنَّا لَمَّا طَغَا ٱلْمَآءُ حَمَلْنَـٰكُمْ فِى ٱلْجَارِيَةِ
textEn: Indeed, when the floodwater overflowed, We carried you [O humans] in the sailing Ark,
textTranslit: Innaa lammaa taghal maaa'u hamalnaakum fil jaariyah

textAr: لِنَجْعَلَهَا لَكُمْ تَذْكِرَةً وَتَعِيَهَآ أُذُنٌ وَٰعِيَةٌ
textEn: so that We might make it a reminder for you, and that attentive ears may take heed.
textTranslit: Linaj'alahaa lakum tazki ratanw-wa ta'iyahaa unzununw waa'iyah

textAr: فَإِذَا نُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ نَفْخَةٌ وَٰحِدَةٌ
textEn: Then when the Trumpet is blown with a single blast,
textTranslit: Fa izaa nufikha fis soori nafkhatunw waahidah

textAr: وَحُمِلَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَٱلْجِبَالُ فَدُكَّتَا دَكَّةً وَٰحِدَةً
textEn: and the earth and mountains are lifted up and crushed with a single blow,
textTranslit: Wa humilatil ardu wal jibaalu fadukkataa dakkatanw waahidah

textEn: on that Day the Inevitable Event will occur.
And the sky will split asunder and will be frail on that day,

textAr: وَٱلْمَلَكُ عَلَىٰٓ أَرْجَآئِهَا ۚ وَيَحْمِلُ عَرْشَ رَبِّكَ فَوْقَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ ثَمَـٰنِيَةٌ
textEn: with the angels on all its sides, and on that Day, eight [mighty angels] will bear the Throne of your Lord above them.
textTranslit: Wal malaku 'alaaa arjaaa'ihaa; wa yahmilu 'Arsha Rabbika fawqahum yawma'izin samaaniyah

textAr: يَوْمَئِذٍ تُعْرَضُونَ لَا تَخْفَىٰ مِنكُمْ خَافِيَةٌ
textEn: On that day, you will be brought forth [before Allah], and none of your secrets will remain hidden.
textTranslit: Yawma'izin tu'radoona laa takhfaa min kum khaafiyah

textAr: فَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِىَ كِتَـٰبَهُۥ بِيَمِينِهِۦ فَيَقُولُ هَآؤُمُ ٱقْرَءُوا۟ كِتَـٰبِيَهْ
textEn: As for the one who is given his Record in his right hand, he will say, “Here is my Record, read it!
textTranslit: Fa ammaa man ootiya kitaabahoo biyameenihee fa yaqoolu haaa'umuq ra'oo kitaabiyah

textEn: I was sure that I would meet my reckoning.”
So he will be in a pleasant life,
in a lofty garden,
with clustered fruit within his reach.

textAr: كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ هَنِيٓـًٔۢا بِمَآ أَسْلَفْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَيَّامِ ٱلْخَالِيَةِ
textEn: [They will be told,] “Eat and drink joyfully for what you did in the days gone by.”
textTranslit: Kuloo washraboo haneee'am bimaaa aslaftum fil ayyaamil khaliyah

textAr: وَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِىَ كِتَـٰبَهُۥ بِشِمَالِهِۦ فَيَقُولُ يَـٰلَيْتَنِى لَمْ أُوتَ كِتَـٰبِيَهْ
textEn: As for the one who is given his Record in his left hand, he will say, “Would that I had not been given my Record,
textTranslit: Wa ammaa man ootiya kitaabahoo bishimaalihee fa yaqoolu yaalaitanee lam oota kitaaabiyah

textEn: nor had I known anything of my reckoning!
Would that it [i.e., death] had been the end of me!
My wealth has been of no avail to me.
My authority has vanished.”
[It will be said,] “Seize him and shackle him,
then make him burn in the Blazing Fire,

textAr: ثُمَّ فِى سِلْسِلَةٍ ذَرْعُهَا سَبْعُونَ ذِرَاعًا فَٱسْلُكُوهُ
textEn: then tie him up with a chain of seventy cubits long.
textTranslit: Summa fee silsilatin zar'uhaa sab'oona ziraa'an faslukooh

textAr: إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ لَا يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ
textEn: For he did not believe in Allah, the Most Great,
nor did he encourage feeding the needy.
So Today he will have no close friend here,
nor any food except the discharge of wounds,
textTranslit: Wa laa ta'aamun illaa min ghisleen

textEn: none will eat it except the sinners.”
I swear by what you can see.
and what you cannot see!
Indeed, this is the word of a noble Messenger,

textAr: وَمَا هُوَ بِقَوْلِ شَاعِرٍ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَّا تُؤْمِنُونَ
textEn: and it is not the word of a poet; little do you believe!
textTranslit: Wa ma huwa biqawli shaa'ir; qaleelam maa tu'minoon

textAr: وَلَا بِقَوْلِ كَاهِنٍ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ
textEn: Nor is it the word of a soothsayer; little do you take heed!
textTranslit: Wa laa biqawli kaahin; qaleelam maa tazakkaroon

textEn: It is a revelation from the Lord of the worlds.
If he had falsely attributed something to Us,
We would have surely seized him by the right hand,
then severed his aorta,

textAr: فَمَا مِنكُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ عَنْهُ حَـٰجِزِينَ
textEn: and none of you could have rescued him.
This [Qur’an] is indeed a reminder for those who are righteous.
And We surely know that some of you will be deniers,
textTranslit: Wa inna lana'lamu anna minkum mukazzibeen

textEn: and it will certainly be a source of regret for the disbelievers.
And this is indeed the absolute truth.
So glorify the name of your Lord, the Most Great.
